title: Chaplaincy turns grief into process
discovery: Loss needs ritual, counseling, and memorialization; otherwise grief stays unprocessed and leaks into cohesion and command burden.
evidence: source_library/55_chaplain_corps_united_states_army.md:15-17 - chaplains provide moral support, grief response, and funerals.
source_library/19_bomb-tank.md:15-17 - replacement personnel turn the tank into a memory vessel.
why: The simulation should store named losses and social consequences, not just casualty counts.

variable.name: grief_load
variable.definition: Accumulated emotional burden from losses and funerals.

pressure: Unprocessed loss makes later decisions worse.
